title: Engineer · Research
summary: You'll be the half of the product team that maps the territory before we build. You'll read the industrial standards, talk to plant engineers, run small POCs to test whether an idea can actually work, and hand clear specs to your pair.

sections.title: What you'll work on
bullets: Read ISA-18.2 end-to-end and turn it into a concrete product spec
Run POCs fast — three approaches in a week, not one perfected in a month
Own the data model: alarm events, asset hierarchies, rationalization rules
Interview reliability managers to understand the real workflow, not the theoretical one
Write the architecture docs your pair builds against
Decide the technical direction with the founder — you're not executing someone else's plan

sections.title: What we look for
bullets: You've built something that required you to figure out the approach yourself
Comfortable with Python, familiar with LangChain and LLM APIs
You read research papers, standards docs, or long-form technical writing for fun
You can argue for an idea, then change your mind when the evidence changes
Bonus: any exposure to industrial data, time-series, or signal processing

sections.title: Leadership & growth
bullets: You'll own the research direction of the product from day one
As we grow, you lead the research side of engineering — hire, mentor, shape the team
You'll learn industrial systems from the inside, not from blog posts

applyBody:
Tell us:

1. Something you've built or worked on that you're proud of
2. What you think we should build next
3. Why this role, for you

Attach your GitHub, writing, or any link that shows how you think. No cover letters.

title: Engineer · Build
summary: You'll be the half of the product team that ships. You take the specs, the POCs, the data model — and turn them into production software that real plant engineers use on Monday morning.

sections.title: What you'll work on
bullets: Build the core web app: data upload, processing pipeline, report generation, customer dashboard
Wire up the LLM pipeline that turns raw industrial data into audit-grade reports
Own deployment, monitoring, and the first customer's production experience
Pair with the research engineer — they spec, you execute, you push back when the spec is wrong
Talk to customers directly. Fix things before they complain
Ship every week. We'd rather have something live than something perfect

sections.title: What we look for
bullets: React or Next.js fluency, Node or Python backend, comfortable with Postgres
A GitHub with projects you actually finished — not just course work
You've shipped something that people used (even 10 people counts)
Taste. You can look at a UI and know what's wrong with it
The instinct to ship, learn, iterate — not to over-engineer and delay

sections.title: Leadership & growth
bullets: You'll own the production codebase of our flagship product from day one
As we grow, you lead the build side of engineering — hire, mentor, set standards
You'll learn how to build software that runs in regulated industrial environments

applyBody:
Tell us:

1. Something you've built or worked on that you're proud of
2. What you think we should build next
3. Why this role, for you

Attach your GitHub, a deployed project, or any link that shows what you can ship. No cover letters.

title: Marketing · Ground
summary: You're the researcher and storyteller of the marketing team. You find the real problems on the ground — by talking to plant operators, reading industry forums, watching what reliability managers complain about online.

sections.title: What you'll work on
bullets: Talk to operators, reliability managers, and plant engineers — collect real problems in their words
Monitor LinkedIn, Reddit, and industry forums for pain points we can build content around
Build the target list: Indian refineries, chemical plants, warehouse DCs with real buyer names
Write the hooks and scripts the video person turns into content
Work with the product team to understand what we built, so you can explain it to customers
Own the CRM, track every conversation, own the handoff to the founder for demos

sections.title: What we look for
bullets: A writer. You know when a sentence is carrying its weight
Natural curiosity about how factories, refineries, and warehouses actually work
Comfortable cold-messaging strangers on LinkedIn and actually getting replies
Resilience — you'll hear no 90 times before a yes, and you'll keep going
Bonus: any B2B research, content, or sales experience

sections.title: Leadership & growth
bullets: You'll own the voice of InquisLab externally from day one
As we grow, you lead the research and content side of GTM — hire, train, scale
You'll learn how to find and sell to industrial buyers — a skill most marketers never get

applyBody:
Tell us:

1. Something you've written, researched, or built that you're proud of
2. What you think we should build next
3. Why this role, for you

Attach writing samples, outreach examples, or any link that shows how you think. No cover letters.

title: Marketing · Video
summary: You're the creative half of the marketing team. You take the problems, hooks, and stories the ground researcher brings you — and you turn them into short videos, posts, and campaigns that get watched.

sections.title: What you'll work on
bullets: Turn raw industrial stories into 30-90 second videos that explain the problem and the product
Use AI video tools (Runway, ElevenLabs, Descript, CapCut AI) to produce fast without a crew
Run the LinkedIn, Twitter, and YouTube accounts — post daily, reply daily
A/B test hooks, thumbnails, and first-3-seconds until something goes viral
Work with the product team to film real product demos that don't look like product demos
Turn customer wins into case-study videos that bring in more customers

sections.title: What we look for
bullets: You've made videos people watched — YouTube, Reels, TikTok, anything with real views
You use AI creative tools and know which ones are actually good right now
A sense of what makes someone stop scrolling
You can ship rough-and-fast today and polish tomorrow — not the other way around
Bonus: any experience making B2B content, or selling something through video

sections.title: Leadership & growth
bullets: You'll own the visual and video output of the company from day one
As we grow, you lead the creative side of marketing — hire, direct, build the content engine
You'll learn how to sell a technical product with video — one of the most valuable skills in modern GTM

applyBody:
Tell us:

1. The best video or piece of content you've made
2. What you think we should build next
3. Why this role, for you

Share links to your work — YouTube, Reels, TikTok, portfolio, anything. No cover letters.
